content.full_text:
October 28, 1977

A 77-12-047

Mr. Edgar L. Stuart
United Steelworkers of America
1070 Concord Avenue, Suite 170
Concord, California 94520

Dear Mr. Stuart:

In response to your letter of October 10, 1977, I hereby
confirm my telephone advice of September 29 to Mrs. Reese of
your office.

You ask whether it is possible to deposit proceeds from
watches sold for fundraising purposes directly into the account
of the International Fund, to which you donate these monies, or
whether they must first be credited to the account of the local
committee, which actually buys and sells the watches.

I assume from the facts that you have provided that both
your local Political Education and Legislative Committee and
your International Political Action Committee are recipient com-
mittees within the meaning of Government Code Section 82011(a).
Therefore, each must report its receipts and expenditures in
accordance with Government Code Section 84210, itemizing trans-
actions with sources and recipients of funds if the transactions
amount to $50 or more on a cumulative basis.

In your case the local unit purchases the watches and must
report the outlay as an expenditure. When the local sells the
watches, it must report the payments received as receipts.
When the local transfers the profits to the International unit,
the local must report the transfer as a payment, and the
International must report the amount received from the local
as a receipt.

(81)

The requirements of the Political Reform Act would not prevent
you from depositing the checks themselves directly into the
International's bank account. However, each committee which raises
funds must report separately its receipts and expenditures and
must keep records showing its involvement in the fundraising pro-
cess. In the instance you have described both committees raise
funds and each must separately report its receipts and expenditures.

Sincerely,


John D. Giansello
Political Reform Analyst

JDG:bw

UNITED STEELWORKERS OF
Subdistrict 3 AMERICA
1070 CONCORD AVENUE, SUITE 120
CONCORD, CALIFORNIA 94520
TELEPHONE (415) 798-7050 ROBERT J. PETRIS, Director

October 10, 1977

Fair Political Practices Commission
Mr. John Gianfello
1100 "K" Street, State Capitol
Sacramento, CA. 95814

Dear Mr. Gianfello:

We have been purchasing watches and pendants for resale
as a fund-raising source. The purchase price has been paid
by our local Coal Political Education and Legislative Committee
(#743842).

Is it possible to deposit the proceeds of the sale
of said items directly into our International Political
Action Committee Fund or since they have been purchased with
local L&E funds, must the funds raised first be credited to
the local L&E Committee for Proposition 9 filing and then
disbursed to the International PAC.

We would like a written statement as to your opinion
on this matter.

Our next L&E meeting will be held on October 22, and
we would like an answer to present to the Committee. Thanks
for your prompt response.

Very truly yours,

Edgar L. Stuart
L&E Coordinator

ELS:bdj
